title: Week
description: Week is a form field for ISO week selection. It supports display formats for date range, week and year, or raw ISO value, plus min and max constraints and a desktop picker panel.
intro: Use `ui-week` when planning, reporting, or scheduling is organized by ISO week instead of a specific date. It is stronger than a plain text field because it can present a week list, enforce valid ISO week values, and keep the chosen week readable in a user-facing format.

featureSections.title: Basic week selection
featureSections.description: The simplest pattern is a single field that stores an ISO week while presenting it in a friendlier visible format.

featureSections.title: Display formats
featureSections.description: The same underlying week value can be shown as a date range, compact week and year label, or raw ISO code depending on the surrounding workflow.

featureSections.title: Min and max constraints
featureSections.description: Constrain the available window when only a subset of weeks is valid for release planning, reporting, or operational cycles.

featureSections.title: Size and field states
featureSections.description: Week behaves like the rest of the field system, so size, readonly, disabled, required, and helper text should be shown in realistic form contexts.

featureSections.title: Reactive form integration
featureSections.description: Validation and submit state usually matter more than the picker itself, so the showcase should include a real reactive form example.

featureSections.title: Planning panel composition
featureSections.description: A practical week field often sits inside a release, sprint, or reporting panel with context tags, status text, and action buttons around it.

accessibility.title: Input and popup behavior
accessibility.body:
`ui-week` behaves like a text field on desktop and can fall back to a native `type="week"` input on mobile when `useNativeOnMobile` is enabled.

| Mode | Behavior |
| --- | --- |
| desktop text input | exposes `aria-haspopup="dialog"` and `aria-expanded` |
| mobile native week input | relies on native platform week input semantics |
| disabled or readonly | blocks opening or changing the value |

accessibility.title: Keyboard
accessibility.body:
Keyboard behavior follows the field and popup model rather than a custom roving picker.

| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `Tab` / `Shift+Tab` | moves through the field and overlay controls |
| `Enter` / click on trigger actions | opens the week picker panel on desktop |
| button activation | selects a week or runs footer actions like clear or this week |

accessibility.title: Labels and guidance
accessibility.body: Use explicit labels such as sprint week, reporting week, or release week so users understand the ISO-week context. When the valid window is narrow, add helper text or nearby status text that explains the allowed range.

apiSections.title: WeekComponent - ui-week
apiSections.lead: Standalone week field built on the shared field system and the date overlay service. It stores an ISO week value while presenting it through a desktop overlay picker or native mobile input.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Controls whether the visible value is shown as a date range, week and year, or raw ISO week.
Switches to a native mobile week input on small screens.

blocks.title: Inherited field inputs
rows: Shared field surface token inherited from `FieldComponent`.
Controls where the field label is placed relative to the control.
Supporting helper text rendered below the field when there is no active error.
Validation or manual error message managed by the shared field base.
Disables the field and its interactive affordances.
Keeps the value visible while preventing user changes.
HTML form name used by the field wrapper and native control wiring.
Control id used to connect label, help text, and error messaging.
Inherited autocomplete hint for native form compatibility.
Inherited shared-field clear-button flag when supported by the concrete control.
Overrides the accessible name when no visible label is appropriate.
Appends extra descriptive ids to the computed `aria-describedby` value.
Enables inherited automatic validation error handling from `FieldComponent`.

blocks.title: Methods
rows: Resets the selected week and underlying value.
Programmatically controls the desktop picker overlay.

blocks.title: Models
rows: Works with `ngModel`, `formControl`, and `formControlName` like the other field components.
Stored ISO week string such as `2026-W20`.

paragraphs: This field is best when the domain model truly works in ISO weeks. If users think in exact dates, a date or calendar component is usually clearer.
The displayed label can differ from the stored value, but the actual written value remains an ISO week string.
